name: Redding, Edward L.
motto: "When Love and Skill Work Together, Expect a Masterpiece."
description: Holder of Territorial record in hammer and discus. Motive in life: To win the fair lady in Cruces. Thesis: Determination of Constant C in Formula for Flow of Water Through Submerged Orifices. Formula Q = C A √2gh.
text:
"When Love and Skill Work Together, Expect a Masterpiece."
Holder of Territorial record in hammer and discus. Motive in life: To win the fair lady in Cruces. Thesis: Determination of Constant C in Formula for Flow of Water Through Submerged Orifices. Formula Q = C A √2gh.

name: Oliver, Ruth Ellen
motto: "But tell me true, will't be a match? Ask my dog, if he say 'aye' it will; if he say 'nay' it will; if he wag his tail and say nothing it will."
description: Motive in Life: To be House Wife of Cornell Graduate. Thesis: Taxation in New Mexico.
text:

name: Boone, Howard Crosby
description: Motive in Life: General Merchandise Merchant, Mesquite, New Mexico. Thesis: Test of a type ten—2 Ingersoll-Rand Air Compressors.
text:
"My hair is my pride."
Motive in Life: General Merchandise Merchant, Mesquite, New Mexico. Thesis: Test of a type ten—2 Ingersoll-Rand Air Compressors.

name: Ames, Walter Roque
motto: "Man delights not me, nor woman, either."
description: MOTIVE IN LIFE: Mayor of Las Cruces.
text:
"Man delights not me, nor woman, either."
MOTIVE IN LIFE: Mayor of Las Cruces.

name: Baker, Stuart Knight
description: Motive in Life: To be Superintendent of the Las Cruces Electric R. R. Company. Thesis: "Some Commercial Tests of Electrical Machinery."
text:
"Silence does not always mark wisdom."
Motive in Life: To be Superintendent of the Las Cruces Electric R. R. Company. Thesis: "Some Commercial Tests of Electrical Machinery."

name: Young, Donald W.
motto: "I am somewhat of an orator but I never do as well as I can out of respect for the memory of Patrick Henry."
description: Democratic Candidate for President. Thesis: History of the Development of the Democratic Party.
text:
"I am somewhat of an orator but I never do as well as I can out of respect for the memory of Patrick Henry."
Democratic Candidate for President. Thesis: History of the Development of the Democratic Party.

name: Fraker, Arthur F.
motto: "It is easier to look wise than to talk wisely."
description: Motive in life: Specialist in Applied Mechanics. Thesis: The determination of Constant C. in formula for flow of water through submerged orifices.
text:
"It is easier to look wise than to talk wisely."
Motive in life: Specialist in Applied Mechanics. Thesis: The determination of Constant C. in formula for flow of water through submerged orifices.

name: Arner, Grose Rede.
description: "What a man am I."
text:
"To be a Missionary to China."
"What a man am I."

name: Mayer, Paul W.
description: On an off day the Juniors elected him President of their class. Unsuccessful in love he takes revenge in slamming two-baggers across the diamond to the infinite delight of all other under-dogs. His record in classes forbids the implying of anything on that score although the taking of that course in which he is registered will probably necessitate his returning in 1912 for stenographic work.
text: On an off day the Juniors elected him President of their class. Unsuccessful in love he takes revenge in slamming two-baggers across the diamond to the infinite delight of all other under-dogs. His record in classes forbids the implying of anything on that score although the taking of that course in which he is registered will probably necessitate his returning in 1912 for stenographic work.

name: Hines, Raye
description: A heart smasher for fair. No sarcasm is included in the preceding; it is merely a general way of stating that at least three have engaged and succumbed in the contest of "bitter sweet." Raye clinched her position in the hearts of her classmates when she entertained them on April 1st. May it be further said to the credit of this estimable young lady that during the present year she served as President of the Y. W. C. A.

name: McCowen, Henry C.
description: Foreman of the "Whoop-em-up" outfit. Spends part of the week getting out the College paper and the balance of the time getting out of the way of those irate victims of his yellow journal who will not accept apologies. He doesn't believe in going with the girls, but he goes just the same. He will make a valiant effort to graduate on time.
text: Foreman of the "Whoop-em-up" outfit. Spends part of the week getting out the College paper and the balance of the time getting out of the way of those irate victims of his yellow journal who will not accept apologies. He doesn't believe in going with the girls, but he goes just the same. He will make a valiant effort to graduate on time.

name: Fitz Gerald, Percy C.
description: He claims that he hasn't time for girls, athletics, journalism or any of the other side shows, but in an emergency he will exhibit a remarkable adaptability to any of the above diversions. Fitz is a farmer of marked ability.
text:
"Slow but sure"
He claims that he hasn't time for girls, athletics, journalism or any of the other side shows, but in an emergency he will exhibit a remarkable adaptability to any of the above diversions. Fitz is a farmer of marked ability.

name: Quesenberry, George R.
motto: "There is a whole lot to him."
description: He is quite high up in the world—at least his head. George has been with the class since 1902; in fact he is all that is left of the original class. He was once President of the "bunch" but resigned for the reason that he found the ceiling an uncomfortable restriction when he had to stand on a six-inch platform.
text:
"There is a whole lot to him."
He is quite high up in the world—at least his head. George has been with the class since 1902; in fact he is all that is left of the original class. He was once President of the "bunch" but resigned for the reason that he found the ceiling an uncomfortable restriction when he had to stand on a six-inch platform.

name: Rigney, Joseph W.
description: J. W. is the latest addition to '11 and certainly one of its most valuable members. He was a dark horse in the debating line, but he proved to be a speedy animal in the race for the inter-collegiate team. Mr. Rigney is an adept in the incubator business and a worthy example of the worthy ruralities.
text: J. W. is the latest addition to '11 and certainly one of its most valuable members. He was a dark horse in the debating line, but he proved to be a speedy animal in the race for the inter-collegiate team. Mr. Rigney is an adept in the incubator business and a worthy example of the worthy ruralities.

name: Campbell, William E.
description: This corpulent individual has been with the class for half a decade. He is from the land of 'Show me' and if you rub his fur the wrong way he exhibits all the characteristics of that animal native to the muddy state. The chief aim of his life is to be a civil engineer, but his main endeavor is concentrated on keeping his avoirdupois below the 200 pound mark. Constant handling of the long green and jingling tin during the last two years, while 'hamburger' disposer and Round-Up manager has earned for him the appellation of 'Chief Grafter.'
text: This corpulent individual has been with the class for half a decade. The chief aim of his life is to be a civil engineer, but his main endeavor is concentrated on keeping his avoirdupois below the 200 pound mark. Constant handling of the long green and jingling tin during the last two years, while 'hamburger' disposer and Round-Up manager has earned for him the appellation of 'Chief Grafter.'

name: Mayer, Bertha
description: From the Sub-Freshman class, Bertha has kept steadily abreast of her classmates and is consequently a full-fledged Junior. She is one of the few girls who believe that a degree from a technical school is worthwhile. In Miss Mayer we find a girl of the reliable, dependable sort and one for whom everyone has something respectful to say.
text: From the Sub-Freshman class, Bertha has kept steadily abreast of her classmates and is consequently a full-fledged Junior. She is one of the few girls who believe that a degree from a technical school is worthwhile. In Miss Mayer we find a girl of the reliable, dependable sort and one for whom everyone has something respectful to say.

name: Powers, John
description: Johnie joined the 'bunch' when it was known as the Junior Prep class. He is Irish and proud of it and possesses all of the race's conversational and oratorical powers. Furthermore, he is the embodiment of first, class, and then college spirit. Of course he is taking an engineering course, but in our generosity we cannot hold that against him. He has never petted the girls, but he often allows them to pet him.
text: Johnie joined the 'bunch' when it was known as the Junior Prep class. He is Irish and proud of it and possesses all of the race's conversational and oratorical powers. He has never petted the girls, but he often allows them to pet him.

name: Sewall, E. Merrill
description: He started out pretty well in his Freshman year, but he has been slowing down until the beginning of the present year when he spied something pretty by the roadside and he almost came to a full stop. He is a man who has no particular aversion for College side shows for he has been everything from Football Manager to Swastika Editor. He has no mean ability, neither has he a single redeeming trait beyond the fact that he is a FARMER.
text: He started out pretty well in his Freshman year, but he has been slowing down until the beginning of the present year when he spied something pretty by the roadside and he almost came to a full stop. He is a man who has no particular aversion for College side shows for he has been everything from Football Manager to Swastika Editor. He has no mean ability, neither has he a single redeeming trait beyond the fact that he is a FARMER.

name: Paul, Brownlee
description: Two years ago he was a Sophomore. During last year he was enrolled in the Freshman class and now, by some miracle, he is a Junior and also the Business Manager of the volume you hold in hand. Before coming here, he was a farmer, and now, to supplement his practical experience, he is taking scientific work. He is therefore a good example of the progressive evolution of the modern agriculturist.

name: Goebel, Gordon Wilson
motto: "Whence and what art thou, execrable shape?"
description: THESIS: Some Commercial Tests of Electrical Machinery.
text:
"Whence and what art thou, execrable shape?"
THESIS: Some Commercial Tests of Electrical Machinery.
